import collections

class FreqStack:

    def __init__(self):
        # Stores the frequency of each value
        self.counts = collections.defaultdict(int)
        
        # Maps frequency to a list (stack) of values that have that frequency.
        # The list acts as a stack to preserve the order of insertion for tie-breaking.
        self.freq_stack = collections.defaultdict(list)
        
        # Keeps track of the current maximum frequency among all elements
        self.max_freq = 0

    def push(self, val: int) -> None:
        # Increment the frequency of the pushed value
        self.counts[val] += 1
        current_freq = self.counts[val]
        
        # Add the value to the stack corresponding to its current frequency
        self.freq_stack[current_freq].append(val)
        
        # Update the overall maximum frequency if needed
        if current_freq > self.max_freq:
            self.max_freq = current_freq

    def pop(self) -> int:
        # Get the list of values that currently have the maximum frequency
        # This list acts as a stack; the last element pushed is the one we want to pop
        val_to_pop = self.freq_stack[self.max_freq].pop()
        
        # Decrement the frequency of the popped value
        self.counts[val_to_pop] -= 1
        
        # If the stack for the current maximum frequency becomes empty,
        # it means there are no more elements with that frequency.
        # So, we decrement max_freq to the next highest frequency.
        if not self.freq_stack[self.max_freq]:
            self.max_freq -= 1
            
        return val_to_pop
